name: Alytus County (Lithuania)

colors.symbolism: the outer field, standard for Lithuanian county flags
the ornate border and the knight of the arms
the golden crosses of the border

description: A blue field within an ornate white-and-gold border, bearing at the centre the coat of arms of Alytus County — a white armoured knight on horseback (the Vytis).
history: Like the flags of all ten Lithuanian counties, the flag of Alytus County follows a uniform template introduced after the country's redivision into apskritys (counties) in 1994: a blue field with an ornate border surrounding the county arms at the centre.

features.symbolism: blue field within a white-and-gold ornamental border in the Lithuanian county pattern

features.role: coat of arms of Alytus County
features.symbolism: an armoured knight on horseback charging with raised sword (the Vytis), in the colours of Alytus
